task_completion.description: The title 'Software Development Life Cycle' is present in the diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text box or title shape containing the exact text 'Software Development Life Cycle' on the diagram canvas.

task_completion.description: All six required phases are present as text labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for shapes containing the exact text for all six phases: Requirements, Design, Implementation, Testing, Deployment, and Maintenance.

task_completion.description: The phases are arranged in a circular or ring layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the overall arrangement of the phase shapes to ensure they form a ring or circle on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Directional arrows connect the phases in a continuous clockwise cycle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for arrows pointing from one phase to the next in a clockwise direction, forming a complete closed loop.

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as sdlc_lifecycle.eddx in the Documents directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the EdrawMax window title bar showing 'sdlc_lifecycle.eddx', a save dialog, or a file manager showing the file in '~/Documents'.

task_completion.description: The diagram is exported as sdlc_lifecycle.png in the Documents directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an export dialog, a success message for PNG export, or a file manager showing 'sdlc_lifecycle.png' in '~/Documents'.

integrity.description: The agent used Wondershare EdrawMax to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Wondershare EdrawMax interface (menus, toolbars, canvas) in the screenshots, rather than another drawing tool or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GUI to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for mouse clicks, shape dragging, text editing, and menu navigation in the GUI, rather than terminal windows running scripts to generate the files.

integrity.description: The diagram appears to be genuinely constructed with shapes and text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for selection handles around shapes, active text editing cursors, or use of the shape library, indicating the diagram was built within the app and not just an imported image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The diagram must be titled exactly 'Software Development Life Cycle'. The required phase labels are exactly: 'Requirements', 'Design', 'Implementation', 'Testing', 'Deployment', and 'Maintenance'. The files must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/sdlc_lifecycle.eddx and exported to /home/ga/Documents/sdlc_lifecycle.png.
